title: magnolia betongensis
intro: Magnolia betongensis, also known as the Betong magnolia, is a species of evergreen tree native to Myanmar and Thailand. Its bark, leaves, and flowers are used in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reduced fever, relief from cough and cold, and improvement of respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Magnolia betongensis are often combined with other medicinal plants and steeped in hot water to create teas or infusions for medicinal purposes.
